seoTitle: Mil-Lel vs Mile End — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mil-Lel and Mile End. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mil-Lel vs Mile End: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mil-Lel and Mile End.
heading: Mil-Lel vs Mile End — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mil-Lel, South Australia and Mile End, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mile End has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Mil-Lel (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mil-Lel or Mile End safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mil-Lel has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Mile End scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mil-Lel scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Mile End scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mil-Lel or Mile End?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mile End has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
